1.1: Sayings of the Preacher the son of David, King of Jerusalem
1.2: Futility of futilities, says the Preacher; futility of futilities, everything is futility!
1.3: What profit has a son of man in all the toil that he toils under the sun?
1.4: A generation goes and a generation comes, and Earth stands to eternity
1.5: The sun rises and the sun sets, and to the place where it rises it is returning; from there it will rise again.
1.6: It goes to the South and is circled to the North and the wind goes to its circuits and the wind returns
1.7: All rivers run sea into the sea and the sea is not filled. The place to which the torrents depart, there they return
1.8: All accounts are wearisome; a man will not be satisfied to speak and the eye is not satisfied to see and the ear is not filled with hearing
1.9: Whatever was, is, and what was done, that is done. and there is nothing new under the sun
1.10: Everyone who will speak and will say, "see, this is new", surely it was from the ages which were before us
1.11: There is no memory for former things, neither will they who are afterwards have memory for later things
1.12: I, The Preacher, have been King over Israel in Jerusalem
1.13: I have given my heart to seek and to understand by wisdom all that is done under the sun; an evil task LORD JEHOVAH gives the children of men to practice in it
1.14: I have seen all the work that is done under the sun, and behold, everything is futility and agitation of spirit
1.15: The disturbed cannot be set in order and the lacking cannot be numbered.
1.16: I spoke with my heart, saying: "behold, I am great and I have increased in wisdom above all who have been before me in Jerusalem", and my heart saw a multitude of wisdom and of knowledge.
1.17: And I gave my heart to know wisdom and knowledge, proverbs and understanding, and I have known that this is also agitation of spirit
1.18: Because with abundance of wisdom is much grief, and he that increases knowing increases sorrow.

2.1: And I said I to my heart: come, "prove yourself with joy and see good", and, behold , this also is futility
2.2: I said to laughter: "What are these things?", and to joy", what have you done? ", and I turned that I would quiet my heart
2.3: I thought in my heart to give pleasure to my flesh with wine, and my heart meditated in wisdom and held to understanding until I would see what good there is for the children men that they may do under the sun for the number of days of their lives
2.4: I multiplied Servants for myself, I built houses for myself , I planted for myself vineyards
2.5: I made for myself gardens and paradises, and I planted in them trees of every fruit
2.6: I made for myself pools of waters to water the woods of fruit trees by them
2.7: I got for myself male Servants and female Servants and I had a multitude of children of houses, and also I had more property of bulls and of sheep than all who were before me in Jerusalem
2.8: I gathered to me also silver and gold and the property of Kings and of cities; I made for myself male Singers and female Singers and delight among the children of man. I made for myself male Cupbearers and female Cupbearers
2.9: And I was great and I had gained more than all that had been before me in Jerusalem, and also my wisdom remained with me
2.10: And all that my eyes asked I have not withheld from them, and I did not withhold my heart from any joy, because my heart rejoiced in all of my labors, and this has been my portion of all my labor
2.11: And I gazed at all the work that my hands had made and at all the labor that I had labored to make, and behold, everything was futility and agitation of spirit, and there is no profit under the sun
2.12: And I gazed to see wisdom and vice and folly, for who is the man that shall enter after the King into judgment, and then with him who made him?
2.13: And I have seen that there is more profit in wisdom than foolishness, as light is greater than darkness
2.14: A wise man’s eyes are in his head and a fool goes in darkness, and I have known that one event happens to them all
2.15: I said in my heart: "surely, according to the misfortune of a fool, it will happen also to me, and why am I more wise? And I said in my heart: "this also is futility!"
2.16: For the fool speaks from excess, because there is no remembrance of the wise with the fool for eternity, like days which already are coming and all are forgotten, and so the wise will die with the fool .
2.17: And I hated life because the work that is done under the sun is evil to me, because everything is futility and agitation of spirit
2.18: I hated all my labor that I have labored under the sun, because I leave it to a man who comes after me
2.19: And who will know if he shall be wise or a fool, and he will be authorized over all my labors that I wrought and when I was made wise under the sun, and this also is futility
2.20: And I turned to convince my heart about every labor that I labored under the sun
2.21: Because there is a man whose labor is in wisdom and in knowledge and in success, and a man who does not labor in it gives him his portion, and this also is futility and great evil!
2.22: And what does a son of man have in all his labor and in the desire of his heart which he wrought under the sun?
2.23: For all his days are sorrow and his work is grief, also his heart at night does not lie down, and this also is futility
2.24: There is no good for a man but to eat and to drink and to show good to his soul in his work; this also I have seen that it is from the hand of LORD JEHOVAH
2.25: For who is going to eat and who will drink apart from him?
2.26: For to the man who is good before him he gives wisdom and knowledge and joy, and to sinners he gives the task to increase and to gather and to give what is good before LORD JEHOVAH, and this also is futility and agitation of spirit

3.1: To everything there is a time, and a time for every matter under the sun
3.2: A time to be born and a time to die, the time to plant and the time to uproot the plant
3.3: A time to kill and a time to save life, a time to tear down and a time to build
3.4: A time to weep and a time to laugh, a time to dance and a time to mourn
3.5: A time to cast away stones and a time to gather stones, a time to embrace and a time to refrain from embracing
3.6: A time to lose and a time to seek, a time to bind up and a time to loose
3.7: A time to rip and a time to sew, a time to be silent and a time to speak
3.8: A time to love and a time to hate, a time of war and a time of peace
3.9: What profit has a son man in the work that he labors under the sun?
3.10: I have seen the task that LORD JEHOVAH gives the children men in which to be employed
3.11: All that he does is beautiful in its time, and he puts eternity in their heart, so that a man will not find the work that LORD JEHOVAH does from the beginning and until the latter end
3.12: I have known that there is no good in them but to gladden and do what is good in their lives
3.13: And also that every man may eat and drink and see good in all his work, this is the gift of LORD JEHOVAH
3.14: I have known that everything LORD JEHOVAH does shall be for eternity; there is nothing to add to it and nothing to subtract from it, and LORD JEHOVAH works that they shall be in awe of him
3.15: Whatever has already been, is, and all whatever shall be, has been for a long time, and God will seek the persecuted who is driven away
3.16: And again I have seen under the sun the place of judgment: there is wickedness, and the place of righteousness: there is evil
3.17: I said in my heart:" the righteous and the wicked God judges, because there is a time for every matter and every work"
3.18: I spoke in my heart about the speech of the children men whom God created , and to show that they are as beasts
3.19: Mishap meets the children men and mishap of beasts; one mishap occurs to all; like the death of one, so dies also the other, and they all have the same Spirit, and there is no superiority of a son of man compared to beasts, for everything is futility
3.20: All go to one place; everyone was from dust and each returns to dust.
3.21: And who knows if the spirit of the children of men ascends above, and the spirit of beasts descends under the earth?
3.22: I have seen there is nothing better for them but that a man rejoice in his works, because that is his portion, for who will bring him to see all that will be after him?

4.1: And I turned and I have seen all the oppressions that are committed under the sun, behold, the tears of the oppressed, and there is no comforter for them from the hand of their oppressors of power, and they have no helper
4.2: And I praised the dead who have died long ago, more than the living, those who are alive now
4.3: But better than both of these is the one who is not yet, because he does not see the evil work that is done under the sun
4.4: I have seen all the labors and all successes of the owners of a work, because the zeal of a man is more than his neighbor’s, and this also is futility and agitation of spirit
4.5: The fool clasps his hands and eats his flesh
4.6: Better a handful in quiet than handfuls with toil and agitation of spirit
4.7: And I turned and I saw futility under the sun
4.8: There is one and there are not two, and he has neither a son nor a brother, and there is no end to all his labor, and also wealth does not satisfy his eyes; and for whom do I labor and deprive my soul of good? And this also is futility and evil business
4.9: Two are better than one, for they have a good reward in their labor
4.10: For if one falls, his companion lifts him up; but grieve for him who is alone, for if he falls, there is no one to lift him up
4.11: And if two will lie down they will get warm, and how will one be warm alone?
4.12: And if one will prevail, two of them will stand against him, and a threefold cord is not quickly broken
4.13: Better a boy who is poor and wise than an old King who is foolish and still does not know how to take warning
4.14: Because from prison he goes forth to reign, for also in his kingdom he was born poor
4.15: I have seen All Life walking under the sun, with The Second Young Man that will arise in his place
4.16: And there is no end to all the people, all who have been before him, neither will the latter ones rejoice in him, because even this is futility and agitation of spirit

5.1: For keep your foot when you go to the house of God, and come near; to hear is better than gifts of the offerings of fools, because fools do not know to do what is good
5.2: Do not be quick with your mouth and your heart will not be quick to send out a word in front of God, because God is in Heaven and you are in the Earth; because of this let your words be few
5.3: Because a dream comes in a multitude of business, and the voice of a fool in a multitude of words
5.4: When you vow a vow to God, do not delay its accomplishment, because there is no pleasure in fools, but pay what you vow
5.5: It is better that you do not vow than that you vow and you do not fulfill
5.6: You shall not give your mouth to cause your flesh to sin and you should not say in front of God: "It is my error", lest God will be angry against your voice and he will destroy the work of your hands
5.7: Because in many dreams and empty things and many words of deception you worship God
5.8: And if you shall see defrauding of the poor and rape and judgment in the city, do not marvel at the matter, because he who is higher than the high watches, and they are exalted over them
5.9: And the profit of Earth in all is a King cultivating a field.
5.10: He who loves silver will not be satisfied with silver and he who loves money will not keep it, and this also is futility
5.11: In the abundance of good its consumers have increased, and what is the profit to its owner except only the sight of his eyes?
5.12: A laborer has sweet sleep, whether he eats little or much, and the fullness of the rich man does not allow him to sleep
5.13: There is a disease of evil that I have seen under the sun: wealth that is kept by its owner to his harm
5.14: And wealth is lost in bad business, for he begets a son and there is nothing in his hand
5.15: Just as he goes forth from the womb of his mother naked, he will return to go as he came, and he will take nothing of his labor to go in his hand
5.16: And this also is an evil disease, for as he comes, so he goes, and what is the profit in all that he labored for the wind?
5.17: And also because he consumes his days in darkness and in much anger and in rage and in mourning and in disease and in rage
5.18: This thing I, the Preacher, have seen: it is good and beautiful to eat and to drink and to see good in all his labor that he labored under the sun, the numbers of the days of his life, the days LORD JEHOVAH gives to him, for that is his portion
5.19: And also every man to whom LORD JEHOVAH gives wealth and property and authorizes him to eat of it and to take his portion and to rejoice in his work, this also is the gift of God
5.20: Because he will not much remember the days of his life, because God occupies him with the joy of his heart

6.1: There is evil that I have seen under the sun, and it is great upon the children of man
6.2: A man to whom God will give wealth and goods and honor and his soul lacks nothing of all that he desires and God does not enable him to consume it, but a man who is a stranger will consume it who comes after him; this is futility and a disease
6.3: If a man will beget a hundred and he will live many years and the days of his years will be multiplied, and his soul will not be satisfied from precious things, and also he will have no burial, I have said that a miscarriage is better than he
6.4: Because he came in futility and in darkness he will go, and in darkness his name will be hidden
6.5: Also he does not see the sun and he knows no rest, one no more than the other
6.6: And if he lives two thousand years and he sees no good, do not all go to one place?
6.7: All that a man labors goes into his mouth and his soul is not full
6.8: Because there is more profit to the wise than to the fool. Why does the poor man know to go to The Life?
6.9: Better the sight of the eyes than the going of the soul, and this also is futility and agitation of the soul
6.10: Whatever has been from the first is called by its name, and it is known what a son of man is, and he cannot judge with whomever is more powerful than he
6.11: Because there are many matters that increase futility. What profit does a son of man have?
6.12: For who knows what good a son of man has in his life, the numbers of the days of life of his futility, and he passes them as a shadow? Who will show a son of man what will be after him under the sun?

7.1: A good name is better than good oil, and the day of death is better than the day of birth
7.2: Better to go to the house of weeping than to go to the banquet house, because this is the end of all the children of men, and The Living One gives good to his heart
7.3: Grief is better than laughter, because in the sadness of the face the heart will improve
7.4: The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning, and the heart of fools in the house of joy
7.5: Better to hear the reproof of the wise than a man who hears the song of fools
7.6: For like the sound of thorns under a metal pot, so is the laughter of fools, and this also is futility
7.7: Because oppression destroys the wise and a gift destroys the heart
7.8: Better is the end of a matter than its beginning, and better is patience of spirit than pride of spirit
7.9: Do not be quick in your spirit to be angry, for anger rests in the breast of fools
7.10: Do not say: "Why is it that the former days were better than these"?, because it is not in wisdom you ask about these things
7.11: Wisdom is better than a supply of arms, and it is greater for those seeing the sun
7.12: Because the shade of wisdom is like the shade of money, and the profit of knowledge is that wisdom will save its owner
7.13: See the work of God, for who is able to restore him that is disturbed?
7.14: In the day of goodness be in prosperity, and in the day of evil look after your soul; God also makes this one opposite that one, so that a son of man will not find anything to say after him
7.15: Everything I have seen in the days of futility: there is a righteous one who is destroyed in his righteousness and there is an evil one who is prolonged in his evil
7.16: Do not be too righteous, and do not be too wise, lest you be stupid
7.17: You shall not be greatly irreverent so that you be not greatly hated; do not be foolish, lest you die when not in your time
7.18: It is good that you take hold of this, neither let your hand go from this, because whoever is in awe of God will cling to all these things
7.19: Wisdom strengthens the wise more than ten rulers who are in the city
7.20: For there is not a righteous man in Earth who will do what is good and will not sin
7.21: And also do not give your heart to all statements that the wicked will utter; do not listen to your servant who insults you
7.22: For your heart knows you also have insulted others many times
7.23: All these things I tested by wisdom; I said: "I shall be wise," and this was far removed from me
7.24: Beyond all that is distant and the depth of depths; who will find it?
7.25: I and my heart have traveled about to know and to perform and to seek wisdom and calculation and to know the wickedness of the fool and foolishness and crime
7.26: And I have found that more bitter than death is a wife who is a snare and her heart a net; she has bound the hands of him who is good; he who is good in front of God is delivered from her, and he who sins is caught by her
7.27: See this that I have found, says the Preacher, one by one, to find reasons
7.28: And again my soul searched and I have not found; one man of a thousand I have found, and a woman among all these I have not found
7.29: However, see this that I have found: "God has made men upright, and they have searched out many schemes"

8.1: Who is like The Wise One and who knows the interpretation of the Word? The wisdom of a man will enlighten his face, and he whose face is shameless will be hated
8.2: The mouth of the King keep, and do not be hasty concerning the word of the oath of God
8.3: Go from before him and do not stand in an evil matter, because all that he will desire he will do
8.4: Just as the King has spoken, he is authorized, and who will say to him: "What are you doing?"
8.5: He who keeps the commandment does not know an evil matter, and the heart of the wise knows time and judgment
8.6: Because for every desire there is a time and judgment, because the affliction of man is great upon him
8.7: Because there is no one who knows what has been, and what will happen to him. Who shows him what comes after him?
8.8: There is no man who is authorized over the wind to withhold the wind, and there is no authority in the day of death, and there is no escape in the day of war, and evil does not save its masters
8.9: And I have seen all this and I have given my heart to know all work that is done under the sun, and the time when a man is authorized over a son of man to afflict him
8.10: And then I saw the wicked being buried, and they came, and they went from the place of holiness and were forgotten in the city where they had thus worked, and this also is futility
8.11: Because punishment of evil doers is not done quickly, therefore the heart of sons of men is filled in them to do what is evil
8.12: Whoever sins does a hundred evils and prolongs himself, and I know that it will be good for the worshipers of LORD JEHOVAH who will stand in awe before him
8.13: And the wicked one will have no good, and he will not lengthen days like a shadow, because he is not in awe before God
8.14: There is futility that is done upon Earth because there are righteous ones to whom it happens as the work of the wicked, and there are the wicked to whom it happens like the work of righteous ones; and I said that this also is futility
8.15: And I praised joy, for there is no good for a son of man under the sun but to eat and to drink and to rejoice, and this accompanies him in his labor all the days of his life that God gives him under the sun
8.16: Because of this, I have given my heart to know Wisdom and to see the business that is done upon Earth, because by day and by night with his eyes he sees no sleep
8.17: And I have seen all the work of God, for a son of man will not master the work that is done under the sun. All that a son of man toils that he may seek he will not find, and none who will claim to know The Wise One will be able to find

9.1: For all these things I have put into my heart, and my heart saw all this: that the righteous ones and the wise and their works are in the hand of God; also a son of man does not know love or hatred; everything that is before him is futility
9.2: All alike happens to all; the same thing to the righteous ones and to the wicked, to the good and to the evil, to the pure and to the defiled, to the sacrificer and to the non- sacrificer, like the good the sinner is also, like the oath taker also is the one who fears oaths
9.3: This evil is in all whatever is done under the sun, because one thing happens to them all, and also the heart of man is full of evil and the evil wandering in their heart, and in their lives, and their end is with the dead
9.4: Everyone who shall be joined to All Life has trust, because a living dog is better than a dead lion
9.5: Because the living know that they are dying, and the dead do not know a thing, and there is no more reward for them, because their memory is forgotten
9.6: Also their love and their hatred and also their envy is now destroyed, and there is no more portion for them for eternity in all whatever is done under the sun
9.7: Come, eat your bread with joy and drink your wine with a good heart, because God is pleased with your works!
9.8: Always let your garments be white and let not your head lack oil
9.9: And see life with the wife whom you have loved all the days of the life of your futility, because she is your lot in life and in your labor that you labor under the sun
9.10: All that your hand finds to do, do in your power, for there is no work or reasoning or knowledge or wisdom in Sheol, because you are going there
9.11: I turned and I saw under the sun that the race is not to the swift , neither to a mighty man the battle, neither to the wise bread and not to the learned riches, and not to the intelligent glory, because time and mishap happens to them all
9.12: For a son of man does not know his time, like fish that are caught in an evil net; like the fowl that are taken in a snare, so are the children of men taken in an evil time when it will fall upon them from the stillness
9.13: This wisdom also I have seen under the sun, and it is great to me
9.14: There was a small city and a few children of men in it, and a great King came to it and he surrounded it and he built bulwarks against it
9.15: And a poor wise man was found in it and that poor man delivered the city by his wisdom, and no person remembered that poor man
9.16: And I said in my heart: "Wisdom is better than might, and the wisdom of the poor is despised and his statements are not heeded
9.17: Words of the wise in quiet are heeded more so than the shout of a prince who is a fool
9.18: Better is wisdom than instruments of war, and one sin destroys many good things

10.1: Like dead flies putrefy a vessel of sweet oil, a little foolishness is weightier than wisdom and than great glory
10.2: The heart of the wise is at his right hand and the heart of a fool at his left
10.3: And also a fool whenever he goes on the road, lacks his heart, and all that he thinks is foolishness
10.4: If the spirit of a prince rises against you, do not leave your place, because healing dissolves many sins
10.5: There is an evil I have seen under the sun, like an error that goes forth before a ruler
10.6: For a fool sits in a high mighty place, and the rich will be seated in humility
10.7: I have seen Servants upon horses and Rulers walking like Servants upon the Earth
10.8: He who has dug a pit will fall into it and he that breaks a hedge, a snake will bite him
10.9: He who removes stones will be hurt by them, and he who splits wood is wearied by it
10.10: If the iron is dull and the surface is rough and it multiplies those killed, also wisdom is a profit for diligent ones
10.11: And if a snake will bite without a charm, there is no profit in a charmer
10.12: The words of the mouth of the wise one are glory, and the lips of a fool ruin him
10.13: The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness, and the end of his mouth is evil shame
10.14: And a fool multiplies words; a son of man does not know what has been, and who shows him what will be after him?
10.15: The labors of fools weary them, for they do not know how to go to the city
10.16: Woe to you, oh city, when your King is a boy and your princes eat at dawn!
10.17: Your blessing, oh city, when your King is a son of the free and your Princes in their time eat with diligence and not with drunkenness!
10.18: By slackness a roof will be lowered and by slackness of hands a house will drop
10.19: For joy, bread and wine are made, and oil that gladdens life; and money humbles and seduces them in all things
10.20: And also do not revile the King in your mind, and in your bedroom do not insult a rich man, because a bird of the sky will transmit your voice, and the Lord of the bird will show your statement

11.1: Send your bread upon the face of the waters, for after many days you will find it
11.2: Give a portion to seven, even to eight, because you will not know what evil may be upon Earth
11.3: If clouds will fill up, they will cast rain upon Earth, and if a tree has fallen unto the South or unto the place of the North, where the tree has fallen, there it will be
11.4: He who watches the wind does not sow, and he who observes the clouds does not reap
11.5: Because you do not know the way of the wind, for as the way of her who is pregnant of conception, so you will not know the works of LORD JEHOVAH, all that he made
11.6: Every dawn sow your seed, and at evening do not remove your hand, because you do not know what will prosper, whether this or that, or if both are good together
11.7: Sweet is the light and good for the eyes, and it is better to see the sun
11.8: Because if a son of man will live many years, may he rejoice in all these and be reminded of the days of darkness, for they will be many. All that comes is futility
11.9: In the days of your youth rejoice, young man, and it will be well for you, and walk in the ways of your heart and in the vision of your eyes, and know that in all these LORD JEHOVAH brings you into judgment
11.10: Remove anger from your heart and separate evil from your flesh, because youth and ignorance are futility

12.1: Remember your Creator in the days of your youth until the evil days will come, and the years will approach when you say: "I have no pleasure in them"
12.2: Until the sun will darken and the light of the moon and of the stars, and the clouds return after the rain
12.3: In the day when the Housekeepers will shake and the Masters of power are agitated, and the grinders will fail because they are few, and the watchers in the windows will grow dark
12.4: And the gates of the streets will be shut by feebleness of the sound of the grinders, and he will rise to the voice of a bird, and all the daughters of song will be brought low
12.5: And also he will be afraid of height and he will shake in his ways, and wakefulness will seek for him and the bitter almond will bud and the locust will multiply and henna plant will be scattered and evil will cease, because a son of man goes to the house of his labor* and mourners go about in the streets
12.6: Until the cord silver is cut and the gold bowl will be shattered, and the pitcher will be shattered over the fountain and the wheel will run over the well
12.7: And the dust will return to Earth like it was and the spirit will return to LORD JEHOVAH who gave it
12.8: Futility of futilities, says the Preacher; futility of futilities, everything is futility!
12.9: And moreover, because the Preacher was wise who had been teaching knowledge to the people, and he listened and he investigated and he has set in order many proverbs
12.10: The Preacher sought much to find statements of pleasure and he wrote in truth statements of truth
12.11: The words of the wise are like goads and like nails with which Masters nail door posts; they are given from one Shepherd
12.12: And especially pay attention to them, my son. To the writing many scrolls there is no end, and much speaking is weariness of the flesh
12.13: The end of the matter in its sum: listen to everything and be in awe before LORD JEHOVAH, and keep his commandments, for this is of One Craftsman which is given to every person
12.14: Because LORD JEHOVAH brings all works into judgment concerning everything that is hidden, and it is revealed whether it is good or evil
